name: Chocolate Decadence Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the broken chocolate with butter in a heavy saucepan, stirring constantly until smooth. Alternatively, microwave on medium for 2 1/2 to 3 minutes, stirring until smooth.
2. Beat egg yolks for a few minutes with 2 tbsp of sugar and vanilla extract.
3. Remove melted chocolate from heat and stir in beaten egg yolks using a wire whisk.
4. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with salt, gradually adding the remaining sugar, beating until soft peaks form.
5. Pour the chocolate mixture into a large bowl containing the beaten egg whites on top.
6. Using a wire whisk, carefully combine the two mixtures without overmixing.
7. Cut parchment paper to fit the bottom of a 9-inch spring form pan. Butter the bottom of the pan and the top of the paper.
8. Spread the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake in an oven set to 350°F on the lower shelf for 30-35 minutes, or until cracked on top and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Cool on a wire rack. The cake will shrink as it cools.
11. Remove the pan and loosen the cake from the paper.
12. For a chocolate-glazed cake, trim the top straight, turn the cake upside down onto a flat cake plate.
13. For whipped cream frosting with raspberry sauce, place the cake on top side up.
Separate recipe
Source: Recipes from Ghirardelli Chocolate Company of San Francisco
From: Sallie Austin
